description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust wooden handle and two large, symmetrical steel blades mounted on either side of the head. The handle is long, cylindrical, and appears to be reinforced with metal bands near the top and bottom, providing both durability and grip. The blades are broad, crescent-shaped, and have a matte metallic finish, with a central mounting block that secures them to the handle. The axe head features a rectangular central section, possibly for added weight and balance, and the blades are sharpened along their outer edges. The overall design is functional yet imposing, suitable for heavy-duty chopping or ceremonial display. The color palette consists mainly of dark brown for the handle and silvery-gray for the blades, with some gold or brass accents at the joints.
description: A double-bladed battle axe with a long wooden handle, reinforced by metal bands and featuring two large, crescent-shaped steel blades. The design is robust and visually striking, with metallic and wooden textures.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large, symmetrical steel blades and the full width of the axe head.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle, with minimal blade visibility.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the back of the blades and the central mounting block.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin edge of the blades and the straight handle.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical wooden handle with metal band reinforcements; no visible curvature or ergonomic grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Double-bladed, crescent-shaped steel heads with sharpened outer edges and a rectangular central mounting block; symmetrical and robust.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible metal bands securing the blade to the handle.
